title: Smithia
taxonomy: Smithia is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This family encompasses a large number of plant species, many of which are nitrogen-fixing due to their ability to form symbiotic relationships with Rhizobia bacteria. Smithia is further categorized into the subfamily Faboideae, which includes approximately 18,000 species. Within the subfamily Faboideae, Smithia belongs to the tribe Millettieae, characterized by the presence of a distinctive type of flower structure. This classification helps to group Smithia with other similar species and provides insight into its evolutionary relationships.

The health benefits of Smithia include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the management of conditions such as arthritis and diabetes.
